checkedConditions: `handle_comm_stop` replays an existing persisted final stop response before target resolution, preventing retries from falling through to target lookup after the member was removed.
`handle_comm_stop` calls `begin_or_replay` before emitting `SessionCloseRequested`, removing live sessions, removing swarm membership, or cleaning channel subscriptions.

Repeated exact coordinator/target stop request after completion replays `Done` with the new request id and does not send another close request to the target event channel, covered by `stop_replay_does_not_send_duplicate_close_request`.
Authorization and target-resolution errors still happen before mutation state is created when there is no persisted final response.
Focused stop tests, swarm mutation tests, cargo fmt/check, git diff --check, selfdev build, and reload passed.
uncheckedConditions: `handle_comm_stop` begins or replays its swarm mutation before any stop side effects, including target `SessionCloseRequested` fanout and session/member removal.
Repeating the same coordinator/target stop request after completion replays `Done` without sending another close request to the target event channel.
Authorization/target-resolution errors remain unchanged and do not create mutation state before the target is known and stop is allowed.
Focused stop idempotency test, cargo fmt/check, git diff check, and selfdev build pass.
evidence: src/server/comm_session.rs now loads a persisted stop final response before resolving the target and moves `begin_or_replay` before `fanout_session_event` and removal side effects.
src/server/swarm_mutation_state.rs exposes `into_server_event` inside the server module so stop can replay final responses by request id.
Selfdev build task 2928649o70 passed and reload succeeded.
Initial broad filter task 089226mvm0 timed out after selecting unrelated stop-substring tests, but the new focused test had already passed and the tighter final verification passed.
notes: Contract output paths were updated to include src/server/swarm_mutation_state.rs because the stop handler needs to convert a persisted response into a ServerEvent before target resolution.
